intended_stay: long-term, residency-seeking; arrives on tourist entry first
narrative: The worldwide-representative relocator: the person this project's public layer actually exists for. $2,500/month clears 7 of 10 candidates on income alone and misses 3 (Mexico's Temporary Resident, Greece's Digital Nomad, USA's categorical absence). $120,000 property capital buys a 2BR in the cheapest documented market, a studio in a moderate one, cannot touch USA or prime-neighborhood CDMX/Buenos Aires/Medellín. Arrives on tourist entry first, residency-seeking thereafter. NOTE (flagged 2026-07-09): income_type='remote-active' is an editorial read for fixture-generation purposes, not a resolved project fact — the locked persona definition states only 'location-independent' and never pins remote-vs-passive. Where this ambiguity is load-bearing (e.g. Guatemala's Rentista-vs-Digital-Nomad route choice), the rescore treats either reading as clearing a real route rather than picking one silently.

intended_stay: long-term, residency-seeking; arrives on tourist entry first
narrative: The retiree - tests income type, not just income amount. $1,900/month pension clears Guatemala Rentista/Pensionado, Colombia Pensionado (income-type condition resolves cleanly for her), Portugal D7, and both ends of Morocco's disputed Carte de Séjour pour Inactif range. Misses Belize QRP and Argentina Rentista by the smallest recorded margins in the project (~5%). Thailand's Non-Immigrant O retirement route becomes available at her age, pending verification. $95,000 property capital is her strongest visa+property convergence (Santa Marta).

intended_stay: open-ended and mobile - months-to-years per place, not residency-seeking on arrival
narrative: The tighter-budget roamer - the persona for whom the tourist/visitor layer is the primary answer, not the footnote. $1,300/month active freelance clears exactly one residency-adjacent visa project-wide (Thailand's DTV) and it is a structural dead end (never converts to PR). Clears Portugal D7's number but fails its passive-income type - the clean proof that scoring rules must formalize income type, not just compare integers. $0 property capital / $25,000 savings: she rents everywhere, exercising the rental-data half of every location's cost-of-living picture.
